jokes.text: If I could sum up the Pawnee Commons in one word, it would be: Dynamite. 'Dynamite' stands for daring, youth-friendly, natural, amazing, merry, inviting, tourist attraction-y, and eco-friendly.
jokes.setup: Setting up to describe something in 'one word'
jokes.punchline: Creating an absurdly elaborate acronym that defeats the purpose of using 'one word'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her over-the-top enthusiasm and inability to be concise. The acronym construction is clever wordplay.

jokes.text: Thank you, Bill. No, please, everyone be seated. Let the record show there was a standing ovation.
jokes.setup: Jamm acting like he received a standing ovation
jokes.punchline: Others immediately contradicting him, revealing his delusion
jokes.explanation: Classic Jamm arrogance and self-delusion, followed by immediate deflation from reality.

jokes.text: History will decide.
jokes.setup: Being told there was no standing ovation
jokes.punchline: Doubling down with mock-grandiose response about 'history'
jokes.explanation: Economical and quotable. Jamm's refusal to admit he was wrong escalated to absurd historical proportions.

jokes.text: We make money. They make money. They make burgers. We eat burgers. That's a win-win-win-win.
jokes.setup: Explaining the benefits of his Paunch Burger proposal
jokes.punchline: Adding nonsensical extra 'wins' to make it sound better
jokes.explanation: Jamm's simplistic thinking and misunderstanding of business terminology. The escalating 'wins' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Love-- love fades away. But things... Things are forever.
jokes.setup: Wedding registry employee giving life advice
jokes.punchline: Cynically suggesting material goods outlast love
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected wisdom from a retail employee. Perfectly deadpan delivery makes it quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: That's beautiful. Did the Dalai Lama say that?
jokes.setup: Hearing the cynical advice about love vs. things
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically comparing it to spiritual wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Ben's dry sarcasm plays well against the registry employee's cynicism.

jokes.text: Ooh! A citrus reamer! Oh, this is the only way to ream citrus. You're gonna need two of these.
jokes.setup: Continuing the registry tour
jokes.punchline: Obsessive enthusiasm about a highly specific kitchen tool
jokes.explanation: Good character work for the obsessive registry employee. The specificity of 'citrus reaming' is amusing.

jokes.text: Yeah, and people could donate, or they could bid to put their names on everything in the park, like benches, cobblestones, trees, ants, air.
jokes.setup: Listing things people could sponsor in the park
jokes.punchline: Escalating from reasonable (benches) to absurd (ants, air)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. Goes from plausible to ridiculous in a satisfying way.

jokes.text: Right, some of that is possible.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting sponsoring ants and air
jokes.punchline: Leslie's measured response suggests some impossible things are actually possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie response - practical but still ambitious enough to consider sponsoring air. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm omelet. Get it? 'I'm on it,' 'I'm omelet'?
jokes.setup: Tom making a food pun
jokes.punchline: Explaining his own pun immediately
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - making puns and then explaining them ruins the joke, but that's perfect for his character.

jokes.text: I get it, and I love it, but I don't have time for food puns right now.
jokes.setup: Tom making food puns during crisis mode
jokes.punchline: Leslie's stressed but polite dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment - even when stressed, she's supportive but focused.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm heading out. Good-pie. Go. Gurt. Go-gurt. I'm incredible.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing with food puns despite being dismissed
jokes.punchline: Multiple puns culminating in self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Tom's self-admiration is the real joke here. His inability to stop making puns even when told to stop.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Gayle. My girls!
jokes.setup: Emergency drill announcement
jokes.punchline: Jerry panicking about his family during a drill
jokes.explanation: Jerry's over-the-top panic about a drill is perfectly in character. His devotion to his family played for comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, this is just a drill, but I am having so much fun pretending it's real.
jokes.setup: Emergency situation declared
jokes.punchline: Ron's oddly enthusiastic response to disaster simulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - finding joy in chaos and disaster scenarios. His deadpan delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Should someone stop Jerry? Gayle! Eh, he'll figure it out eventually.
jokes.setup: Jerry still calling for his family
jokes.punchline: Everyone deciding to just let him keep panicking
jokes.explanation: Good running gag with Jerry's panic. The group's casual dismissal of his distress is darkly funny.

jokes.text: First, we do the written exam, and then we do the personality evaluation, which I feel like I'm gonna nail, 'cause people always say, 'But he's got a great personality.'
jokes.setup: Andy confident about personality evaluation
jokes.punchline: Not realizing 'great personality' is usually a backhanded compliment
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent misunderstanding of a common insult. His obliviousness is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Assault and battery. The way I remember that, I picture 35 a-salt shakers and 42 batteries.
jokes.setup: Andy being quizzed on police codes
jokes.punchline: Absurd mnemonic device using literal salt and batteries
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy logic - taking the pun literally and creating an elaborate visual. Both clever and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Arson is 533. You know how I remember that? I picture 533 salt shakers and batteries lighting a building on fire.
jokes.setup: Continuing the mnemonic device pattern
jokes.punchline: Escalating to an even more absurd scenario
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the previous joke. Andy's commitment to his absurd system creates a perfect running gag.

jokes.text: And if you don't, I'll just divorce you and marry someone else and then cheat on them with you.
jokes.setup: Trying to encourage Andy about his test
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely convoluted romantic backup plan
jokes.explanation: Peak April darkness disguised as support. The logic is twisted but perfectly her - romantic yet threatening.

jokes.text: I found a dead rabbit on the side of the road, and I cut its feet off and made it into a lucky charm.
jokes.setup: April giving Andy a lucky charm
jokes.punchline: Revealing the grotesque DIY nature of the gift
jokes.explanation: Perfectly April - macabre but somehow sweet. The casual delivery of horrific content is her specialty.

jokes.text: Baby, you are so creepy. Thank you. I love it.
jokes.setup: Receiving the dead rabbit feet charm
jokes.punchline: Andy being genuinely touched by the creepy gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy-April dynamic. His sincere appreciation for her darkness makes their relationship work.

jokes.text: I am Leslie Knope. I am the Pawnee Emergency Czar. And this year, we are more than prepared for your test, but, um, it just so happens, today is a little inconvenient.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing herself to the emergency coordinator
jokes.punchline: Immediately admitting the timing is inconvenient
jokes.explanation: Leslie's inability to hide her inconvenience despite trying to appear professional. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Good, because the best day for a drill is when it's inconvenient for everyone. My mother is getting a colonoscopy today. I'm not even sure there's anyone there to drive her home, so we're all making sacrifices, Ms. Knope.
jokes.setup: Leonard responding to Leslie's inconvenience complaint
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his mother's medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Unexpected overshare creates awkward humor. Leonard's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Went past the zero. I got to wait till it goes... Okay, now.
jokes.setup: Leonard trying to start the drill with a stopwatch
jokes.punchline: Fumbling with the timing mechanism
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy with Leonard being less than professional. Moderately amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Wait. Maybe this is a nightmare. Nope, can't fly away. This is real life.
jokes.setup: Leslie trapped and hoping it's a nightmare
jokes.punchline: Testing dream logic by trying to fly
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy moment. Leslie's desperation leads to childlike dream logic testing.

jokes.text: Uh, correction. That's 'Mission Im-Pawnee-able: Knope Protocol.'
jokes.setup: Someone reading the emergency protocol name
jokes.punchline: Leslie's elaborate pun-based title
jokes.explanation: Peak Leslie - turning emergency planning into wordplay opportunities. The pun is elaborate and very her.

jokes.text: No offense, Ron, but I don't think you'd be great on TV. I can speak in full sentences, and I won't cry. Fair point. I did cry last time.
jokes.setup: Ben doubting Ron's TV abilities
jokes.punchline: Ron's confident response followed by Ben's admission
jokes.explanation: Good character moment and callback. Ron's confidence contrasted with Ben's self-awareness about his TV failures.

jokes.text: I'm from Orlando. And I don't care about the Paunch Burger. If it was another French restaurant, maybe I'd worry.
jokes.setup: Tom appealing to his 'refined Parisian sensibilities'
jokes.punchline: Chef revealing he's from Orlando and doesn't see Paunch Burger as competition
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - deflating Tom's assumptions about the chef's background and motivations.

jokes.text: Hey! Well, you know what? What? What you got for me? Get out of my kitchen! Okay, okay, okay. I'm sorry, sorry, sorry. Pardon! Pardon!
jokes.setup: Chef getting aggressive with Tom
jokes.punchline: Tom's panicked retreat with French phrases
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Tom getting kicked out. The French phrases add character consistency but it's fairly standard.

jokes.text: Right. So how do I kill 'em-- like, with a gun? I could fill up a bathtub and just drown 'em one at a time.
jokes.setup: Brett asking about killing birds for the drill
jokes.punchline: Suggesting increasingly disturbing methods
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from Brett taking the drill too seriously. His enthusiasm for animal violence is disturbing but funny.

jokes.text: A few months ago, the thought of an infectious disease, even hypothetical, would have sent me careening towards Bummerville, but now I am infected with a killer virus, and I feel fine. Therapy!
jokes.setup: Chris being told he's infected in the simulation
jokes.punchline: Using it as evidence of his therapeutic progress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character moment - his relentless optimism applied to death. The 'Bummerville' reference is quintessentially him.

jokes.text: That is impossible. I do not ride the bus. I ride my bicycle behind the bus as a windbreak.
jokes.setup: Chris being accused of riding the bus
jokes.punchline: Revealing his bizarre cycling technique
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor. Chris's fitness obsession taken to illogical extremes. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Yeah, listen, I'm a little under the weather. I went on a booze cruise last week. It just finished up an hour ago.
jokes.setup: Joan explaining why she's sick
jokes.punchline: Claiming a week-long booze cruise just ended
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joan excess. The absurd timeline of a week-long booze cruise is hilarious and very her character.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Joan Callamezzo. Today's show will not go on.
jokes.setup: Joan starting her show
jokes.punchline: Immediately announcing it won't continue
jokes.explanation: Joan's professional instincts warring with her hangover. Short, sweet, and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Hey, Joan, settle a bet for me. Who's the sexiest couple in history? R-Patz and K-Stew, or Bieber--
jokes.setup: Caller asking about celebrity couples
jokes.punchline: The shallow nature of local TV call-in shows
jokes.explanation: Standard satire of mindless celebrity culture on local TV. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I don't know how to answer that. A simple 'yes' or 'no.' Well, everyone calls me Andy, but my full name is Andrew, I think, so... No? Wait. Yes.
jokes.setup: Simple question about Andy's name
jokes.punchline: Andy overthinking and confusing himself about his own identity
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy confusion. Making the simplest question complicated through overthinking. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, first, I would be like, 'Dad... You're alive? What the hell? Also, do you know where my catcher's mitt is?'
jokes.setup: Scenario about pulling over his father
jokes.punchline: Andy revealing daddy issues and childhood concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment revealing his backstory through inappropriate priorities. The catcher's mitt detail is perfect.

jokes.text: She has a bad hangover, which she is pretending is allergies. Wouldn't know-- never been hungover. After I've had too much whiskey, I cook myself a large flank steak, pan-fried in salted butter. I eat that, put on a pair of wet socks, and go to sleep. That works? It does.
jokes.setup: Explaining Joan's condition and hangover cures
jokes.punchline: Ron's bizarre but confident hangover prevention method
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson content. The wet socks detail elevates it from standard 'Ron loves meat' to truly bizarre and memorable.

jokes.text: Please call now if you have questions about tonight's gala or one of my other interests-- Woodworking, uh, novels about tall ships, meat, that sort of thing.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to redirect calls to the gala
jokes.punchline: Listing his personal interests as alternative topics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron interests list. The casual 'that sort of thing' undersells his very specific passions beautifully.

jokes.text: After I've had too much whiskey, I cook myself a large flank steak, pan-fried in salted butter. I eat that, put on a pair of wet socks, and go to sleep.
jokes.setup: Ron explains he's never been hungover
jokes.punchline: His hangover cure involves an elaborate steak dinner and wet socks
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Ron Swanson - the steak is perfectly in character, but the wet socks detail is brilliantly unexpected. The combination of macho and bizarre is perfect.

jokes.text: I, Chris Traeger, after several sustained hours of diarrhea, combined with violent coughing and a devastating fever, followed by even more diarrhea, have succumbed to the avian flu. I'm dead.
jokes.setup: Chris reading his death announcement
jokes.punchline: Maintaining his enthusiastic delivery while describing his demise
jokes.explanation: Chris maintaining his positive energy while describing graphic death. The contrast between tone and content is perfect.

jokes.text: Oh, really? Specifically asked that the drill to be done today and said I should give you everything I got.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about the drill intensity
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jamm orchestrated the whole thing
jokes.explanation: Good plot revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode. Makes Jamm's villainy more satisfying.

jokes.text: It is with a heavy heart that I say, 'We have been jammed.' God, that guy is the worst!
jokes.setup: Discovering Jamm's sabotage
jokes.punchline: Leslie's dramatic announcement using Jamm's name as a verb
jokes.explanation: Leslie's dramatic flair applied to wordplay. 'Being jammed' as a concept is memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Great. Why don't you tell the hospital director to flush them down the toilet.
jokes.setup: Leslie deciding to sabotage her own drill performance
jokes.punchline: Ordering the destruction of flu vaccines
jokes.explanation: Leslie's competitive nature turning destructive. The casual way she orders vaccine destruction is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Chief Fugleberg, I want you to order your officers to find all the infected birds in the area and perform CPR.
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing to sabotage the drill
jokes.punchline: Ordering impossible medical procedures on birds
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation. Bird CPR is both impossible and ridiculous, perfect for Leslie's sabotage.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Pawnee has been hit with... A tornado quake!
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing to make the drill worse
jokes.punchline: Inventing an impossible natural disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to the absurd. 'Tornado quake' is a nonsense disaster that sounds almost plausible.

jokes.text: Take a walnut and rub it into the legs of your table. That'll mask the scratches. Next thing you want to do is ditch the Terrier and get yourself a proper dog. Any dog under 50 pounds is a cat, and cats are pointless.
jokes.setup: Caller asking about furniture repair
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical advice followed by his dog philosophy
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron wisdom. The dog size classification system is instantly quotable and perfectly logical for his character.

jokes.text: Banks are Ponzi schemes run by morons.
jokes.setup: Ron continues his random advice stream
jokes.punchline: His anti-establishment views expressed as financial advice
jokes.explanation: Ron's libertarian worldview expressed with characteristic bluntness. The absolute certainty makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I've seen three movies in my life-- Bridge on the River Kwai, Patton, and Herbie Fully Loaded.
jokes.setup: Ron admits to limited movie experience
jokes.punchline: After two war classics, he mentions a Disney comedy about a sentient car
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up Ron's predictable masculine movie tastes then completely subverts it. The contrast between war epics and children's comedy is brilliant.

jokes.text: Everyone in Pawnee is dead. Including Councilman Jamm. What? No, no, you can't do that. It says right there, you're dead, so is everyone you care about. Oh, well, joke's on you. I don't have anyone I care about.
jokes.setup: Leonard announcing everyone is dead
jokes.punchline: Jamm's sad admission that he has no one to care about
jokes.explanation: Dark character revelation disguised as defiance. Jamm's isolation becomes unexpectedly sad and funny.

jokes.text: I can't hear you. I'm a ghost. Yeah, well, so am I, so you can hear me! Ghost jammed!
jokes.setup: Leslie and Jamm both being 'dead' in the drill
jokes.punchline: Jamm applying his signature move to the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the 'jamming' concept to supernatural levels. Jamm's commitment to his brand is perfect.

jokes.text: I also helped a child perform a tracheotomy on his elderly uncle.
jokes.setup: Ron mentions it's been a rewarding day
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions assisting in emergency surgery involving family members
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and disturbing information delivered with Ron's typical matter-of-fact tone. The casual mention of child-performed surgery is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: He got 100% on his written test-- first guy in history to do it-- but he failed his personality examination.
jokes.setup: Andy's test results
jokes.punchline: Perfect academic performance but complete personality failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy paradox - brilliant but completely unsuitable. The historical achievement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Official police wrist lock. You can't hit me. Try to hit me. Or here, no, try-- it's this hand. Wrist lock. Boom, too much pain, you can't even hit me. Try to hit me. If I had my gun, you wouldn't try to hit me, though, is the thing.
jokes.setup: Andy demonstrating police techniques
jokes.punchline: Confusing himself while trying to show off his knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy physical comedy. His enthusiasm outpacing his competence. Slightly verbose but true to character.

jokes.text: ♪ Park, we will build it, the park ♪ it's not built yet, the park ♪ soon there will be a park ♪
jokes.setup: Mouse Rat performing at the gala
jokes.punchline: Incredibly literal and unpoetic lyrics about the park
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy/Mouse Rat songwriting - completely literal and unimaginative but earnest. Very character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Have you ever thought of being a sperm donor? I'm impotent. Yeah, I don't need to know that.
jokes.setup: Leonard asking about sperm donation
jokes.punchline: Immediate overshare followed by regret
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward overshare humor. Economical but not particularly innovative. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Have you ever thought of being a sperm donor? I'm impotent.
jokes.setup: Chris asks Leonard about sperm donation
jokes.punchline: Leonard immediately shuts it down with TMI medical information
jokes.explanation: Chris's inappropriate suggestion meets Leonard's overshare. The abrupt medical revelation creates awkward comedy.
